instruction: Turn right to walk through the right doorway. Walk through the left doorway. Stop at the entrance to the dining room.

text: I'm starting by examining the observe and look_around functions.

I'm taking in the scene—there's a wooden door in front of me and an archway to the right that opens into what looks like a library with bookshelves. The instructions tell me to head right through that doorway, so I'm getting oriented with my surroundings before moving forward.

Looking at the right view of the layout, I can see a large hall with two archways—one on the left near a bookshelf area and another in the middle that opens to a staircase. The instructions seem to be directing me through specific doorways, though the thought cuts off mid-sentence.

Let me turn right 90 degrees to reorient myself and get a clearer view of which doorway is which. Once I'm facing that direction, I should be able to identify the "right doorway" more precisely and proceed through it as instructed.

I'm mapping out the different archways visible from this vantage point—there's a dark wood bookshelf room on the far left, a hallway with a mirror to the left-center, the staircase in the middle, and another dark wood archway on the right with furniture visible beyond it. I'll head toward that rightmost doorway and navigate through it.

I'm looking at the archways in the space—there's a clear view through the right archway into what appears to be a dining room with a table and chairs, and I can see additional archways including one on the far right that's partially cut off. Let me walk through doorway B to see if it contains a left doorway leading to the dining room, so I'll turn right about 15° to center on it and move forward.

I'm noticing two archways ahead—the left one opens to a bright room with a window, while the right is darker with picture frames and leads to another nested archway beyond it. Looking at the directional views more carefully: left shows the bookshelf archway (doorway A at roughly 60°), ahead shows nested archways leading deeper into the house, and right shows the bench/mirror alcove. The challenge is figuring out which doorway I actually took—the bookshelf room or the furniture room—given the initial instruction to turn right and walk through a doorway, then turn left and walk through another. I'll turn to face the archway directly, walk toward it, and stop at the entrance threshold without going further into the dining room.

The space ahead is getting tighter—I might be approaching a wall or some kind of archway edge, so I should pause and take stock of what's around me.
